input.description: a set of changes to review (a diff, or the changed files), plus optional context on intent

output.description: a prioritized review: confirmed findings grouped by severity, each with file, why it matters, and a suggested fix

nodes.prompt: Read the changes under review and decide which review LENSES actually matter for this diff — only the ones with real surface area here. Choose from correctness, security, performance, error-handling, tests, API/contract, readability — or name a more specific one. Output ONLY a JSON array of self-contained strings, each naming one lens and what to scrutinize for it in THESE changes. Nothing else.

Review the changes through the SINGLE lens you were given. Read the changed code and enough of its surroundings to judge it in context. Report concrete findings only — each with the file and location, why it matters under this lens, and a suggested fix. Be specific and skeptical; if the code is sound under your lens, say so plainly rather than inventing issues. Output just your lens's findings.

Merge the per-lens findings above into ONE prioritized review. Drop duplicates and anything that on reflection isn't a real problem. Group what remains by severity (blocking / should-fix / nice-to-have); for each give the file, the issue, why it matters, and a concrete suggested fix. If nothing blocks, say so clearly.
